rulings.Case Title: EDWARD KOUNTZE ET AL VS. KALLE KARL-HEINZ PIEPER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Defendants Motion To Set Aside December 20 2021 Entry Of Default
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 26, 2022, line 2 DEFENDANTS KALLE PIEPER, JOSE MONTOYA, PAOLO BROGGI Motion To Set Aside December 20 2021 Entry Of Default is GRANTED. No opposition filed. = (501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JONES MEMORIAL HOMES, INC. VS. MARK ANTHONY SMITH
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate January 20, 2022 Order Sustaining Defendant Mark Smith'S Demurrer To Plaintiff'S First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 26, 2022, line 3 PLAINTIFF JONES MEMORIAL HOMES, INC. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate January 20, 2022 Order Sustaining Defendant Mark Smith'S Demurrer To Plaintiff'S First Amended Complaint Plaintiff's Motion to Vacate Order Sustaining Defendant's Demurrer is DENIED. Plaintiff failed to attach a copy of its proposed opposition to the demurrer to his motion to vacate; nor was Plaintiff diligent in seeking relief. (CCP 473(b).) Plaintiff waited more than one month after becoming aware of the adverse order before coming to Court to seek relief. Finally, Plaintiff's entire motion is based on bare accusations against Defendant of "fraud" on the Court and "intentional" misconduct. There is no evidence of misconduct, and therefore, no grounds upon which to grant the motion. = (501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BANNEKER HOMES, INC. VS. ROBERT JOHNSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Defendant'S Motion To Vacate Default Judgment And Recall And Quash The Writ Of Execution
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, April 26, 2022, line 4 DEFENDANT ROBERT JOHNSON'S Motion To Vacate Default Judgment And Recall And Quash The Writ Of Execution Defendant's Motion to Vacate Default Judgment and Recall and Quash Writ of Execution is DENIED. There is no evidence of mistake inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect.
